problem: If a school bus leaves school with 48 students on board, and one-half of the students get off the bus at each of the first three stops, how many students remain on the bus after the third stop?

classified_questions.question: Can you identify the initial number of students on the school bus?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many students will get off at the first stop, considering that one-half of the students are getting off?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: After the first stop, how many students will be on the bus?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: At the second stop, how many students will get off, based on the same one-half reduction rule?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: After the second stop, how many students will remain on the bus?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Following the same pattern, can you determine how many students will get off at the third stop?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Finally, after the third stop, how many students will be left on the bus?
classified_questions.label: b
